description_long: This is a butterfly knife, also known as a balisong, featuring a black, perforated blade and two red metallic handles. The blade is straight with several large circular cutouts, likely for weight reduction and aesthetic appeal. The handles are elongated, with matching cutouts and a latch mechanism visible at the end of one handle for securing the knife in a closed position. The red handles appear to be made of anodized metal, providing both durability and a striking visual contrast to the black blade. The overall construction suggests a lightweight yet sturdy tool, commonly used for flipping tricks or as a compact utility knife. The design is modern and utilitarian, with no visible decorative ornamentation.
description: A butterfly knife with a black, perforated blade and red metallic handles, featuring a latch mechanism and a modern, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: The knife is partially open, showing the black blade with circular cutouts and both red handles, one of which displays the latch.
description_view_1: The knife is viewed from the opposite side, highlighting the alignment of the handles and the full length of the blade.
description_view_2: The knife is seen from the back, emphasizing the handle cutouts and the slim profile of the blade.
description_view_3: The knife is viewed from above, clearly showing the blade's perforations and the parallel arrangement of the handles.
attribute_handle_design: straight, anodized aluminum, red, with cutouts and latch
